commands.review_summary:
The PR adds diagnostics around swallowed failures in gateway registry loading, setup checks, command response delivery, chat state recovery, connectivity probing, and audio cleanup, plus regression tests for corrupted state files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the covered source paths: corrupted `gateways.json` and corrupted last-chat-state JSON are directly exercised by added tests in the PR diff. I did not run artifact-producing tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is appropriate because the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a concrete code defect.

target.title: Harden diagnostics for swallowed production failures
target.body:
## Summary
- Ran `slopwatch --no-baseline` and triaged the 312 SW003 empty-catch findings instead of blanket-cleaning legacy/test noise.
- Added targeted diagnostics for production swallowed failures that can affect reliability or debuggability: command failure response delivery, corrupted gateway registry loading, setup keepalive/process checks, audio capture cleanup, chat state recovery, and connection health probes.
- Preserved existing fallback behavior: these paths still continue/return null/return false where they did before; they now leave useful diagnostics.
- Added regression coverage for corrupted `gateways.json` and corrupted last-chat-state JSON.

## Slopwatch
- Before: 312 SW003 findings.
- After: 302 SW003 findings.
- Remaining findings are intentionally left for separate baseline/noise review; this PR only takes high-confidence production hardening cases.

## Validation
- `slopwatch --no-baseline`
- `./build.ps1`
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Connection.Tests/OpenClaw.Connection.Tests.csproj --no-restore`
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.SetupEngine.Tests/OpenClaw.SetupEngine.Tests.csproj --no-restore`
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore`
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore`
